items.title: Perez wijst absolute uitblinker aan bij NEC: 'Snap dat Ajax hem wil'
items.summary: NEC is de grote verrassing van het seizoen. De ploeg van trainer Dick Schreuder strijdt volop mee om plek twee. Analist Kenneth Perez ziet een duidelijke uitblinker.

items.title: Anderlecht ontslaat trainer, Lookman naar Spanje
items.summary: De tijd van winterse versterkingen is aangebroken, in dit blog volgen we alle transferactiviteit in het buitenland. Bekijk hier ons blog voor de transferperikelen in Nederland.

items.title: PSG ontsnapt dankzij penaltykiller aan puntenverlies, ondanks rood Hakimi
items.summary: Paris Saint-Germain is op bezoek bij Strasbourg ontsnapt aan puntenverlies. De koploper van de Ligue 1 leek zondagavond op een gelijkspel af te stevenen, maar trok in de slotfase alsnog aan het langste eind, ondanks een rode kaart voor voor Achraf Hakimi.

items.title: VI Live: Athletic Club komt langszij in Baskische derby
items.summary: Welkom in de VI Live van zondag 1 februari. Hier nemen we je mee in drie Eredivisie-duels, een topper in Engeland en alle andere hoogtepunten op de (inter)nationale velden.

items.title: Volendam gaat Pauwels terug naar Nederland halen
items.summary: De winterse transfermarkt is geopend. In dit blog vind je alle speculaties en bevestigde transfers in Nederland. Bekijk hier ons blog voor de transferperikelen in het buitenland.

items.title: Saint-Maximin leaves América after kids 'abused'
items.summary: Former Newcastle winger Allan Saint-Maximin has left Mexican side Club America by mutual consent after claiming that his children were the victims of racist abuse.

items.title: Sources: Chelsea recall Sarr, send out Anselmino
items.summary: Chelsea will recall Mamadou Sarr from his loan at sister club Strasbourg and sent Aaron Anselmino to France in a swap deal before the end of the transfer window on Monday, sources have told ESPN.

items.title: Arsenal win inaugural Champions Cup, $2.3M prize
items.summary: Caitlin Foord struck in extra-time to see Arsenal past Corinthians in the final of the Champions Cup.

items.title: Copy of Carrick's calm amid the chaos is exactly what Man ...
items.summary: Michael Carrick has three wins from three games in charge at Man United.

items.title: Copy of Premier League title-race twist: Questions for Man...
items.summary: The momentum has shifted in the Premier League title race again as Man City wasted a two-goal lead at Tottenham. How did that happen?

items.title: Foord sees off Corinthians in extra time to put Arsenal on top of the world
items.summary: Final: Arsenal 3-2 Corinthians (aet) Smith 15, Wubben-Moy 58, Foord 104; Zanotti 21, Albuquerque 90+6 pen The most decorated women’s club in England made more history at the Emirates stadium on Sunday night, Arsenal securing a 3-2 win over the Copa Libertadores champions Corinthians in extra time to see them crowned winners of the inaugural Fifa Women’s Champions Cup. They were made to work for their victory, the Brazilian side twice coming from behind to force another 30 minutes of football, but it was somewhat of an inevitability. The Uefa Champions League winners benefited from being mid-season with players at full fitness, in contrast to Corinthians being in their pre-season and Concacaf Champions Cup winners Gotham FC in their off-season, and from the decision to hold the tournament in London, and play the final at the Emirates. This was a competition set up for European success and Arsenal delivered. They are officially the world’s best club and they have a nice trophy to prove it. The 13-point gap, albeit with a game in hand, between them and WSL leaders Manchester City though, says otherwise. Continue reading...

items.title: Hits and misses: Man City get just rewards for sloppy second halves
Solanke scorpion kick helps Spurs fight back against Man City
items.summary: Dominic Solanke scores twice, including a spectacular scorpion kick, as Tottenham come from behind to draw against Manchester City with a rousing second-half performance.

items.title: Sesko scores dramatic late winner as Man Utd beat Fulham
items.summary: Manchester United avoided Fulham's late comeback due to a Benjamin Sesko goal in stoppage time to give United three points and continue head coach Michael Carrick's perfect start.

items.title: Sarr earns Palace draw at Forest as Williams sees red for handball
items.summary: Neco Williams is sent off for deliberate handball in Nottingham Forest's draw with Crystal Palace, as he punched Jefferson Lerma's goalbound header off the line.

items.title: 10-man Brentford hold on for hard-fought win at Villa
items.summary: Brentford hold on to an important win against Aston Villa, despite Kevin Schade's sending off during the first half.

items.title: Pep: Controversial first Spurs goal created emotional issues for us
items.summary: Manchester City boss Pep Guardiola suggested that Dominic Solanke's first goal should not have stood after his side dropped more crucial points in the title race in their draw with Tottenham.

items.title: Familiar tale of two halves haunts Manchester City as Spurs find belated resolve | Jonathan Wilson
items.summary: City again needlessly threw points away but Solanke showed what Tottenham, with their long injury list, have been missing There are times when football is gloriously silly, times when the logic of your eyes and all your experience tells you one thing is happening, and then it turns out the reality is quite different. What seemed at the break as though it was going to be an easy away win unexpectedly became a draw and, as a result, both ends of the table looked quite different at the final whistle to how it appeared they were going to look at half-time. It was a case of multiple immutable but incompatible laws running into each other. On the one hand, Tottenham are terrible and have picked up only 10 points at home this season. But on the other, City have developed a habit of needlessly squandering points and somehow always do worse than expected against Tottenham. The consequence was a game that simultaneously made very little sense but at the same time was predictable, at least in the way it remained true to those fundamental principles. Continue reading...

items.title: Is Carrick proving Amorim's Man Utd tenure was a wasted year?
items.summary: A third win in a row for Michael Carrick highlights the failures of Ruben Amorim's time at Manchester United.
